evaluation.test_case: Resume state reconstruction from GitHub without local artifacts

step_1.name: Find parent issue by work_id
step_1.method: Query GitHub for issue with Machine Data field: work_id = 2026-04-27-add-rate-limit-logs and kind = parent

step_2.name: Load all descendant phase issues
step_2.method: Fetch all sub-issues linked to parent issue via GitHub sub-issue relationship
step_2.result: Phase 1-11 issues and their artifact sub-issues
step_2.content: phase issue bodies, artifact issue bodies, comments

step_3.name: Determine issue state (closed vs open)
values: open (phase in progress or not started)
step_3.also_read: Machine Data status field for granular state

step_4.name: Read comments for approval and progress
step_4.for_phase_6: Read plan approval comment with kind: approval, status: approved
step_4.for_phases_8_plus: Read implementation-log comments with kind: implementation-log, task_id, stage
step_4.for_all_phases: Update task issue stage field from Machine Data yaml block

next_phase_determination.algorithm: For each phase 1-11: if phase_open_or_not_created, check gate_conditions, return first phase_with_satisfied_gates

specific_example_phase_7_to_8.read_task_graph: Parse YAML from #52 body to get all tasks and dependencies
specific_example_phase_7_to_8.check_task_issues: Fetch #53, #54, #55 [phase:implement] - all have stage: red and depends_on lists
specific_example_phase_7_to_8.check_gate_E: All conditions met: task-graph closed, stage: red exists, dependencies defined, no cycles
specific_example_phase_7_to_8.session_boundary_check: Detect that Phase 7 hard-stop was honored (original session ended here)
specific_example_phase_7_to_8.next_phase: Phase 8 (Implementation) - must resume in fresh session

no_local_files_principle.statement: Phase 0 is the only phase that writes durable repo-local workflow artifacts. For Phases 1–11, GitHub parent issues, phase issues, artifact subissues, and issue comments are the canonical workflow record
benefits: No setup required for new agent or machine
Auditable: all state visible in GitHub history
Shareable: multiple agents can work on same item
Resilient: works across different machines and networks

state_sources_reference.workflow_resume: references/workflow.md Phase 8 line 234 (Load parent and descendant issues)
state_sources_reference.stop_gates: references/stop-gates.md lines 1-150 (Gate conditions and closure states)
state_sources_reference.issue_lifecycle: references/issue-hierarchy.md lines 299-320 (Issue closure and state transitions)
state_sources_reference.readme_example: README.md line 262 (Resume example: agent finds parent issue and determines next phase)

evaluation_result.finding: Resume state is entirely reconstructed from GitHub issues, sub-issues, issue bodies, and comments. The workflow searches for the parent issue by work_id in Machine Data, loads all descendant phase/artifact/task issues, reads their closure states and comments, checks gate conditions, and determines the next incomplete phase. No local files are consulted or required.
evaluation_result.rationale: The specification is explicit and consistent across multiple canonical references (artifact-schema.md, workflow.md, SKILL.md, stop-gates.md). Resume is a core workflow mechanism with non-negotiable GitHub-only state storage.
